title: Atrisi system
content:
{{Top|fa|canon=Kitel Phard system}} {{System |image=[[File:KitelPhard-TEA.jpg]] |name=Atrisi system
{{C|Depicted as "Kitel Phard system"}} |region=*[[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' **[[Core Worlds Security Zone]] {{C|[[137 ABY]]}} *[[Southern Core]] |sector=[[Atrisian Commonwealth/Legends|Atrisian Commonwealth]] |coordinates=J-12 |xyz= |quadrants= |suns= |orbits=[[Atrisia/Legends|Atrisia]][[Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''Imperial Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] |stations= |asteroids= |comets= |nebulae= |routes=[[Giju Run/Legends|Giju Run]] |other= |species= |otherspecies=[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language= |population=100–500 million {{C|[[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]]}} |imports= |exports= |affiliation=*Atrisian Commonwealth *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] *[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|Look at the Atrisi system, for example. The principal planet still recalls the Kitel Phard Dynasty with pride.|Major Arhul Hextrophon|}} The '''Atrisi system''', or the '''Atrisian system''', was a part of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] known as the [[Atrisian Commonwealth/Legends|Atrisian Commonwealth]]. It lay on the [[Giju Run/Legends|Giju Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] and contained the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Atrisia/Legends|Atrisia]], [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of a race of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] known as [[Atrisian]]s. Atrisia was unified by the [[Atrisian Empire]] under the leadership of the [[Kitel Phard Dynasty]]. The inhabitants of the Atrisi [[Star system/Legends|system]] subsequently held the dynasty in high regard, and the planet was alternatively referred to as "Kitel Phard." At some point, the [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] parameter known as the [[FD number]] saw prominent use in the Atrisi system. Following the development of interstellar spaceflight by the Atrisians, their home system became a part of the Atrisian Commonwealth, and later a member of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and its successors, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and eventually, [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]]. ==Description== The Atrisi [[Star system/Legends|system]], also referred to as the Atrisian system,{{DB|organization|theempire|Empire, the}} was located in the [[Atrisian Commonwealth/Legends|Atrisian Commonwealth]], a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] in the [[Southern Core]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. The principal [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the Atrisi system was the terrestrial [[Atrisia/Legends|Atrisia]], also known as Kitel Phard. The [[Star/Legends|star]] system lay at the endpoint of the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Giju Run/Legends|Giju Run]], which, after a short detour through the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]], connected it to the Core Worlds' [[Dahrtag system/Legends|Dahrtag system]]. Additionally, a set of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] routes led beyond the Atrisi system, connecting it to a tangle of stars, and ultimately, the astronomical object [[Irkalla]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Irkalla system/Legends|Irkalla system]] ==History== ===Early history=== {{Quote|At the end of the march, the youths assembled at the North Garden. There, brigade leader Nenevanth Tion ''[…]'' recited a speech from the Kitel Phard Dynasty of the Atrisi system.|Republic HoloNet News report|{{InsiderCite|70|Republic HoloNet News Core Edition 14:9:04}}}} [[File:AtrisianCommonwealth-TEA.png|thumb|left|300px|The Atrisi system eventually became a part of the Atrisian Commonwealth.]] At some point, the [[Atrisian|inhabitants]] of Atrisia established a pre-spaceflight civilization on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] that consisted of several nations on multiple continents. The [[Atrisian Empire]],{{JournalCite|14|From the Files of Corellia Antilles}} under the leadership of the [[Kitel Phard Dynasty]], eventually unified Atrisia. The inhabitants of the planet honored the dynasty for a long [[Time/Legends|time]] to come, and Atrisia was subsequently given its alternate name "Kitel Phard." The Atrisians eventually traveled beyond their home planet, which became one of the approximately 200 [[Colonist/Legends|settled]] worlds known as the Atrisian Commonwealth. The area of space surrounding the Atrisi system was [[Scout/Legends|explored]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] at some point between [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] and [[3996 BBY]] and thus became part of what was considered the civilized [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Contact with the independent Atrisian Commonwealth led to the growth of the Southern Core, a region of the Core Worlds and the Colonies. ===Part of the wider galaxy=== {{Quote|The FD number system rose to prominence in the Artisian ''[sic]'' system ''[…]''|An Imperial document included in a report by Major Arhul Hextrophon|}} [[File:CoreWorldsSecurityZone-TEA.png|thumb|right|300px|By 137 ABY, the Atrisi system was a part of the Core Worlds Security Zone.]] Although space surrounding the Atrisi system was a part of the territories either unaligned with or contested by the Republic or the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] by [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], Atrisia fell within the borders of the Republic during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] of [[22 BBY/Legends|22]]–[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. During the first campaigns of that conflict, the [[Twentieth Army]] of the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]] was tasked with defending Atrisia's vicinity against the [[Confederacy military/Legends|military forces]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Shortly after the acquisition of the planet [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]] by the [[Commerce Guild/Legends|Commerce Guild]] during the Clone Wars, the Atrisi system was featured on a [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Map/Legends|map]] displayed on a [[Viewscreen/Legends|viewscreen]] at a facility run by [[Passel Argente/Legends|Passel Argente]], the [[Magistrate/Legends|Magistrate]]''[[The Clone Wars: Act on Instinct]]'' of the [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]].{{DB|character|passelargente|Argente, Passel}} The Kitel Phard Dynasty of the Atrisi system was also mentioned in a [[HoloNet News/Legends|Republic HoloNet News]] report on the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] [[21 BBY/Legends|14:9:04]]. Atrisia lay within space controlled by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]], and at some point by the time the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] took place, the use of the [[FD number]], a parameter characterizing the combat performance of military [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]], rose to prominence in the Atrisi system. The star system was mentioned by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Arhul Hextrophon/Legends|Arhul Hextrophon]] in a compilation of Imperial documents for Alliance [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and other Rebel [[Officer/Legends|officers]]. Following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Atrisia declared its independence from the Empire, and the Atrisian Commonwealth eventually joined the Empire's successor government, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], as an [[Allied Region]]. By [[137 ABY]], the Atrisi system had been incorporated into the [[Core Worlds Security Zone]], an administrative territory of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]]. ==Inhabitants== Atrisia, the principal planet of the Atrisi system, was colonized by [[Human/Legends|Humans]], who were generally thought to have evolved on the Core Worlds planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Despite eventually achieving interstellar spaceflight, the Atrisians remained an insular culture, although some left their homeworld for the wider galaxy, such as [[Rom Mohc]],{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/writtenword/index.html?page=1|text=The Written Word|int=The Written Word}} who served with the Republic Military during the Clone Wars.{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}} The population of the Atrisi system around [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] numbered from 100 million to 500 million. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Atrisia's antiquity and apparent importance to galactic culture argued for putting it in the Core Worlds; we stuck it out on the frontier to give it more of a whiff of the exotic and in hopes that future Star Wars creators might find intriguing possibilities in that.|Jason Fry|{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2009/08/endnotes-for-star-wars-essential-atlas_24.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: The Essential Atlas (part 2 of 5)}}}} [[File:Impsourcebook.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Atrisi system was first mentioned in the ''Imperial Sourcebook''.]] The Atrisi system was introduced in the ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'', a [[1989]] supplement written by [[Greg Gorden]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the name of the system was in one instance misspelled as "Artisian system,"''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' whereas the entry of the original [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] for the Galactic Empire, which was published at some point by [[June 24]], [[2001]], introduced the alternate name "Atrisian system." In the Appendix section of the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' and in the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book,{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} as well as on galactic maps included in ''The Essential Atlas'' and the [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', the Atrisi system was referred to by the name of its principal planet, Kitel Phard. When writing ''The Essential Atlas'', the authors [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] placed the planet Atrisia in the Core Worlds region of the galaxy due to previously published sources noting the planet's antiquity and its cultural importance to the wider galaxy. However, they also placed it on the very frontier of that region as part of grid square J-12—near the border of the explored galactic territories. This was done intentionally to make Atrisia seem a more exotic location and to provide the authors of future ''[[Star Wars]]'' media with interesting storytelling possibilities. "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, had previously placed the Atrisi system in the [[Harron sector/Legends|Harron sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}} it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.{{UnlicensedStatus}} Ultimately, ''The Essential Atlas'' overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the Atrisi system, and therefore Atrisia, was situated outside of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] in which the Harron sector was later placed.{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-expansion-region|text=Essential Atlas Extra: Expansion Region}} ''[[The Clone Wars: Act on Instinct]]'', a [[Star Wars comic books|webcomic]] authored by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and released on StarWars.com over the span of the 2009{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/news20091001comics/index.html|text=New ''Clone Wars'' Web Comic Tells Original Tale}}–[[2010]]{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode222.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Lethal Trackdown}} [[The Clone Wars: Season Two|second season]] of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series, featured the first appearance of the Atrisi system. ==Appearances== *''[[The Clone Wars: Act on Instinct]]'' {{1st}} {{C|Appears on screen only}} *{{InsiderCite|70|Republic HoloNet News Core Edition 14:9:04}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' {{1stm}} *[[Imperial Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''Imperial Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{ID|Kitel Phard system}} *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} {{ID|Kitel Phard system}} *{{DB|organization|theempire|Empire, the}} {{1stID|Atrisian system}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' {{ID|Kitel Phard system}} *[[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Core Rulebook]] {{ID|Kitel Phard system}} *''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File Collector's Guide & Galaxy Chart]]'' {{ID|Kitel Phard system}} Rebellion Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Age of Rebellion'' Core Rulebook]] {{ID|Kitel Phard system}} *[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]]
